== Edit Request: Change infobox location of death ==

Change infobox location of death.

Old: "| death_place = [[Tel al-Sultan]], Rafah, Palestine"

New: "| death_place = [[Tel al-Sultan]], [[Rafah]], [[Gaza Strip]]"

This would reflect the convention for other similar pages for recently assassinated Hamas officials in the Gaza Strip, such as [[Ayman Nofal]], [[Jamila Abdallah Taha al-Shanti]], [[Marwan Issa]] etc. The status of Gaza Strip as part of the [[State of Palestine]], or as part of the autonomous [[Palestinian Territories]] (with the State of Palestine not recognized by most Western or OECD countries), is contentious (it does have limited observer state status in the UN). See [[International recognition of the State of Palestine]]. [[User:Neutral Editor 645|Neutral Editor 645]] ([[User talk:Neutral Editor 645|talk]]) 00:24, 22 October 2024 (UTC)

:{{partly done}}. I have added Gaza Strip as the death location, but I will leave Palestine in until further discussion indicates a more uniform consensus. I have also added a dubious template to help gather consensus more easily. [[User:Wikiexplorationandhelping|Wikiexplorationandhelping]] ([[User talk:Wikiexplorationandhelping|talk]]) 16:03, 22 October 2024 (UTC)
::That seems like a measured and Wikipedian approach. Thank you! [[User:Neutral Editor 645|Neutral Editor 645]] ([[User talk:Neutral Editor 645|talk]]) 22:51, 22 October 2024 (UTC)
::As Israel Controlled such areas at the time i think we should put israeli occupied Gaza like for Sinwar's birthplace [[User:Kingdom of Valoria|Kingdom of Valoria]] ([[User talk:Kingdom of Valoria|talk]]) 21:41, 10 January 2025 (UTC)
::Clowns all of you PALESTINE [[Special:Contributions/47.227.201.0|47.227.201.0]] ([[User talk:47.227.201.0|talk]]) 02:59, 1 July 2025 (UTC)
:Agreed—since this is such a controversial topic, and it is the subject of so many disinformation campaigns, there needs to be more nuance on the part of editors to reserve judgment so as to be conscientious about the fact that information wars cost human lives, no matter your personal opinion. States, for example, do not gain their sovereignty by popular vote of the UN membership. [[User:Kandbsoalkan|Kandbsoalkan]] ([[User talk:Kandbsoalkan|talk]]) 01:33, 23 October 2024 (UTC)
:“Gaza strip” is not a country, but the state of Palestine is. It doesn’t matter if the western client states of Israel do not recognise Palestine considering that most of the world’s countries, including many western ones do in fact recognise it
:The Gaza Strip is part of a country called Palestine, and Yahya sinwar died in the Gaza Strip, which in Palestine, so he died in Palestine  [[User:The Great Mule of Eupatoria|The Great Mule of Eupatoria]] ([[User talk:The Great Mule of Eupatoria|talk]]) 14:46, 26 October 2024 (UTC)
::Your entire reference to Western sovereign nations as "client states of Israel" suggests a very un-Wikipedian bias in this discussion, and it also borders on classically antisemitic conspiracy theories of world domination, domination of the West etc. See the IHRA's [[Working definition of antisemitism]]. [[User:Neutral Editor 645|Neutral Editor 645]] ([[User talk:Neutral Editor 645|talk]]) 17:57, 8 November 2024 (UTC)
:::@[[User:Neutral Editor 645|Neutral Editor 645]] As you are not yet extended confirmed, please remember to limit yourself to edit requests with regards to contentious topics. [[User:Butterscotch Beluga|Butterscotch Beluga]] ([[User talk:Butterscotch Beluga|talk]]) 20:01, 8 November 2024 (UTC)
:::There is absolutely nothing ‘antisemitic’ about calling western nations, with the biggest emphasis on the United States who cannot even bring itself to ''condemn'' this colonising usurping entity, vetoes any attempt to place any consequences on it, and spends billions of dollars so it can keep blowing up kids a ‘client state’.
:::Wikipedia is meant to be neutral. Wikipedia users are not neutral, this is why we have talk pages to discuss contentious topics. That being said, saying that Yahya sinwar was killed in Palestine is not a violation of neutrality  [[User:The Great Mule of Eupatoria|The Great Mule of Eupatoria]] ([[User talk:The Great Mule of Eupatoria|talk]]) 16:00, 20 November 2024 (UTC)
::::I came here from the infobox note which currently reads, “Tel al-Sultan, Rafah, Gaza Strip, Palestine.” That seems fine to me and seems to address all issues. That is unless you want to endlessly beat the drum — and the dead horse — of the "client states of Israel." Please give it a rest. --01:59, 4 December 2024 (UTC) [[User:Giacomo1968|Giacomo1968]] ([[User talk:Giacomo1968|talk]]) 01:59, 4 December 2024 (UTC)
::A [[State (polity)|state]] whose very existence is disputed. You could perhaps try "Occupied Palestinian Territory" [[User:Shoshin000|Shoshin000]] ([[User talk:Shoshin000|talk]]) 17:28, 4 December 2024 (UTC)

== Extended-confirmed-protected edit request on 3 March 2025 ==

{{edit extended-protected|Yahya Sinwar|answered=yes}}
To the infobox:
* Add "order = 4th"
* Add "[[Khaled Mashal]] (acting)" to predecessor [[User:KZ737|KZ737]] ([[User talk:KZ737|talk]]) 08:51, 3 March 2025 (UTC)
:  {{partly done}} Hi  [[User:KZ737|KZ737]]. I added [[Khaled Mashal]] but I didn't write acting because [[Hamas temporary committee]] is acting. Also, I have no clue where to put "order = 4th" and what it means, nor did you provide any source for this. Friendly, [[User:Lova Falk|Lova Falk]] ([[User talk:Lova Falk|talk]]) 14:06, 9 March 2025 (UTC)
::Hi [[User:Lova Falk|Lova Falk]]!
::You're right that the temporary committee is acting ''now'', but Mashal was acting chairman for some days after the [[assassination of Ismail Haniyeh]], before Sinwar's chairmanship. For reference, see the same office in the infobox at [[Ismail Haniyeh]].
::I really didn't specify the order request, my bad. It should be put between the "caption" and the "office" tags, and it means that he was the 4th chairman. I don't have a source specifically for this number, but we have him listed as 4th in [[List_of_leaders_of_Hamas#List_of_chairmen_of_political_bureau]], and we have the orders in the infoboxes of the first 3 chairmen as well, so I think it would make sense to add it here. Kind regards, [[User:KZ737|KZ737]] ([[User talk:KZ737|talk]]) 15:47, 9 March 2025 (UTC)
::: [[User:KZ737|KZ737]] I added order = 4th. I'll take your word for it. I also checked some of the other members of the Hamas temporary committee, and there it is also written (acting), so I have added (acting) behind his name as well. Now everything in your request is... {{done}} [[User:Lova Falk|Lova Falk]] ([[User talk:Lova Falk|talk]]) 16:01, 9 March 2025 (UTC)

== The town? ==

This article draws heavily - perhaps too heavily - from the New York Times article,

https://web.archive.org/web/20240527131142/https://www.nytimes.com/2024/05/26/world/middleeast/hamas-sinwar-israel-doctor-prison-swap.html

The following paragraph (under Early activities and imprisonment) is problematic; the ambiguous reference to "the town" is hardly encyclopedic.
It might be better to quote Sinwar directly - “We aren’t asking for the town” - or to remove the sentence altogether:

"Sinwar's sole interview with an Israeli television outlet in 2005 saw him warning Israelis to "be scared" of Hamas's election victory. However, he privately conveyed that much depended on the Israeli government's actions. He emphasized Hamas' demand for rights from the Israeli leadership, not control of the entire town." [[Special:Contributions/82.2.111.160|82.2.111.160]] ([[User talk:82.2.111.160|talk]]) 14:39, 25 June 2025 (UTC)

:Good catch! I rephrased the passage. [[User:Alaexis|Alaexis]]<sub>[[User_talk:Alaexis|¿question?]]</sub> 15:25, 25 June 2025 (UTC)
==his wife==
she left the strip
https://www.ynet.co.il/news/article/bkasrfc8ee#autoplay
:I have added this information to the article. There is this paragraph in the article saying: "On the streets of Gaza, the sensations become an open rage: “They send their children to study in Turkey and Qatar – and our children to the graves,” says an Azat young man who survived the recent bombings. “What is the difference between them and a corrupt ruler in a dark Arab country? They only care for themselves.” This makes the wife's escape from Gaza relevant, but I don't know if this text somehow should be added to this article or to another one. I'm in a bit of a loss here. [[User:Lova Falk|Lova Falk]] ([[User talk:Lova Falk|talk]]) 09:15, 26 July 2025 (UTC)
:I put it in [[Criticism of Hamas]]. 😊 [[User:Lova Falk|Lova Falk]] ([[User talk:Lova Falk|talk]]) 15:09, 26 July 2025 (UTC)
